dailyJudgementSnippet: Microsoft Copilot edged out the day on the cleanest direct product praise: in the NixOS thread, a commenter said Copilot is "really good" for working with Nix configs, and no front-page discussion pushed back on Copilot itself [E4].
winnerExplanation: Microsoft Copilot won because its only meaningful Hacker News signal was directly favorable and product-specific, while the other tracked providers either had weaker incidental mentions or picked up offsetting criticism in coding-related discussion [E1][E3][E4].

openai.judgementSnippet: Codex only surfaced in the NixOS discussion, where a commenter treated it as one of the coding agents they would trust on NixOS because configs are auditable and rollbackable; that was favorable but incidental rather than a broad product verdict [E3].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude drew both use and frustration: one coding thread said Claude-generated compiler work showed little novelty and described Claude Code repeatedly falling back to conventional patterns, while the NixOS thread offered a smaller lift by saying Claude becomes workable when changes are easy to inspect and roll back [E1][E2].

microsoft_copilot.judgementSnippet: GitHub Copilot got a modest lift from the NixOS thread, where a commenter explicitly said it is "really good" at working with Nix configuration; there was no broader Copilot discussion, so the signal stayed light but positive [E4].

evidence.summary: In 'Reports of code's death are greatly exaggerated,' top comments used Claude as the example of AI coding limits, saying Claude-generated compiler code had nothing innovative and that Claude Code kept reverting to conventional but unwanted solutions.
In 'Why I love NixOS,' a commenter said they would trust Claude to make system changes on NixOS because declarative configs can be audited and rolled back safely.
The same NixOS discussion gave Codex a mild favorable mention by grouping it with coding agents that become acceptable when the OS makes changes auditable and reversible.
In 'Why I love NixOS,' another commenter said Copilot is really good with NixOS-related configuration work, the clearest direct positive mention of a tracked assistant on the front page.
